BM25 is a probabilistic ranking function used by search engines to estimate the relevance of documents to a given search query. It is based on the probabilistic retrieval framework developed by Robertson and Sparck Jones.

The BM25 algorithm scores documents using term frequency and inverse document frequency. It is particularly effective for lexical matching where exact keyword overlap matters.

In enterprise RAG systems, BM25 is often combined with dense vector retrieval to form hybrid search. The lexical matching from BM25 complements semantic similarity from embeddings.
